entry.title: Re: Dynamic parallel processing in SAP S/4HANA flexible w...
entry.content:
What also sometimes helps in custom workflow dev is the Programming Exit on activity Level.

*How do the program exits work?
*To change alternatives of the user decision at runtime dynamically (for each *work item), you can define an ABAP class here that supports interface *IF_SWF_IFS_WORKITEM_EXIT.

*At runtime, method IF_SWF_IFS_WORKITEM_EXIT~EVENT_RAISED is called multiple *times. Here you can define source code that is run for different time points *of the work item processing.

*To delete or change the alternatives, your code must be run at the time *point SWFCO_EVENT_BEFORE_DECISION.

*Example: Class CL_SWH_T_DECISION_EXIT from workflow definition WS77400143 "WF_Verify141"

Method IF_SWF_IFS_WORKITEM_EXIT~EVENT_RAISED.
  data lt_decialts type IF_WAPI_WORKITEM_CONTEXT=>swrtdecialts.
  if im_event_name = IF_SWF_IFS_DECISION_EXIT~C_EVTTYP_BEFORE_DECISION.
    call method IM_WORKITEM_CONTEXT->get_decision_alts
       importing et_decialts = lt_decialts.
*   --- hide alternative 0001 and 0002 ---
    delete lt_decialts where altkey <> '0003'.
    call method IM_WORKITEM_CONTEXT->set_decision_alts
       exporting it_decialts = lt_decialts.
  endif.
endmethod.

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics In-Between Release on May 31st, 2024
entry.content:
A few days ago, there was an ‘in-between’ release for SAP Business Network for Logistics. On the What’s New overview in SAP Help Portal, it is just two entries, but it marks an important milestone. These functions are the first evidence of the convergence of the previously separate networks in the areas of procurement and supply chain collaboration, assets, and logistics.

From now on, maintenance of company profile and onboarding of carriers and data contributors will be done on the new platform serving as the foundation of the SAP Business Network. This is the starting point for many integration scenarios that our customers are waiting for.

So, what has changed exactly?

1. Your company profile has moved to a new platform

All roles in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration as well as SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace, i.e. shippers, solution owners, carriers, data contributors or any other logistics service providers are affected.

The business profile has been merged with the Trading Partner Profile in SAP Business Network. When you open the ‘Manage Business Profile’ application, you will automatically be directed to the new profile.

Fields you had before are also available in the new profile (with minor changes that I will explain in this blog). In addition, there are new fields and functionality e.g. enhanced logic on mandatory fields and options to maintain more details around additional addresses and contacts.

1.1 Overview Section and Business Data

In the former ‘Manage Business Profile’ application, there was a header section and tabs for more details. On the new platform, there is an overview section and a navigation pane on the left. The overview section contains similar information to the former header section (except for LBN ID – see below 1.4).

On the new platform, business data is part of the ‘Overview Section’. Some information such as revenue, founded, employees, … is part of the top section. Below there is more business data such as product and service categories, ship-to-service-location, and industry.

 Please note: the code list for ‘Industries’ has changed. You might have to refill it with a value from the new code list.

1.2 Additional Addresses Section

In the former business partner profile, addresses were stored in a table under the tab ‘Contact Details’. Now, there is a new section for ‘Additional Addresses’ – addresses other than the main address which is part of the overview section - such as warehouses, distribution centers, etc..

When you edit additional addresses, you might notice that there are validation checks embedded for some fields e.g. VAT ID. You can also maintain contacts specific to a particular address.

Navigate to SAP Help Portal for more details on how to create additional addresses.

1.3 Contacts Section

As with additional addresses, there is also a separate section for general contacts such as regional logistics managers, transportation planners, etc.. You can access it via the navigation pane on the left to create, edit or delete contacts.

 Navigate to SAP Help Portal for more details on how to manage contact information.

1.4 Finding your LBN ID

The LBN ID is required for you to be identified correctly in the network. It is not displayed on the new platform. However, you can find your LBN ID in the ‘Systems Connections’ application under ‘Manage Alternative IDs’.

 

1.5 Privacy Settings (Carriers only)

Carriers can decide if they want to make their profile visible to potential business partners and if they would like to automatically accept invites. These settings used to be part of the ‘Manage Business Profile’ application but have now been moved to the application ‘Manage Invitations’. Under ‘Settings’, you have the option to change the privacy settings. Per Default, both are set to ‘no’.

 

1.6. Summary regarding the new platform for business partner profile

The key benefits of having a new and central place for business profiles across all areas of SAP Business Network, procurement, supply chain collaboration, assets and logistics:

  • improved user experience
  • utilize one single profile to showcase your information and reflect organizational structures
  • additional functionality and profile fields
  • make your business profile accessible to potential partners and relevant stakeholders (planned roadmap item)

However, admin data (created by, changed by, …) from SAP Business Network for Logistics has not been migrated. Instead, the new platform has its own admin data. The fields are filled automatically.

And please take note of the following:

  • LBN ID is not displayed in the new profile. You can find the LBN ID in the ‘System Connections’ application.
  • The former privacy settings for carriers ‘make profile visible to potential business partners’ and ‘automatically accept connection requests’ have not yet been transferred, but have been moved to the ‘Manage Invitation’ app in SAP Business Network for Logistics 
  • For some entry fields, there are new code lists available with a mismatch in values (i.e. not all old values can be mapped 1:1 to new values). Please check the following field and potentially refill them with a value from the new code list:
    • Gross Revenue in USD
    • Employee Strength
    • Industry
    • Geographical Coverage
  • The ‘Additional Addresses’ section has added value through validation checks on a number of fields such as VAT ID (e.g. mandatory for Italy), TAX ID, GST ID (e.g. specific information required for locations in Singapore).

For more details on ‘Manage Business Profile’ go to our documentation on SAP Help Portal.

 

2. Onboarding of Carriers and Data Contributors

The account activation process has changed for new carriers that want to onboard to SAP Business Network for Logistics. The process has been simplified! Companies do no longer have to go via SAP Store.

Now, as a logistics service provider:

1.) Receive an invitation from a shipper or solution owner.

2.) Accept the invite by clicking the link in the email.

3.) Registration page opens.

4.) Click on ‘Create Account’.

 5.) Verify the auto-populated email address and proceed by editing (some information prefilled from shipper)/ creating your account.

 6.) Receive email on successful account creation.

7.) Sign in and create your business profile by clicking on the ‘Manage Business Profile’ application (for further details see above section 1).

8.) Configure additional technical users and add users to your account.

More details on Getting started with SAP Business Network for Logistics via new onboarding process can be found in our documentation on SAP Help Portal.

Please note: This process is currently only available if the shipper or solution owner uses the data center for Europe (EU10). However, if the invitation you received was sent out before release 2405 or the sender of the invitation uses a data center other than EU10, you still need to go through the previous account activation process. For more information, see Getting Started (Prior to Release 2405).

 

Please share and amplify our new feature!

---------------------

The above summarizes the May 31, 2025 ‘in-between’ release. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: Re: API Anomaly Detection in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
We're in very early state of discovering CALM.

API-Manger is in general only exposing very little data to CLAM - so it's hard to get an end-to-end view in CLAM including API-Manger.

My question/idea was mainly to have notifications from CLAM instead of using Notification Service framework. Also having all monitoring/observability in one place also, this would also enable teams to monitor multiple systems and stage from a single point.

From the anomaly detection in general, at least all anomalies should be forwarded to CLAM so the system status in CLAM could at least change to some warning state. Having data like what is normal load what was the data during anomaly would for sure be nice as well, but this might mean to replicate the dashboard completely to CALM.

entry.title: Record Mobile Services Network Trace Using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools
entry.content:
In the previous blog post we know how to analyze the mobile services network trace har file using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools, actually you can record the network trace using mobile backend tools directly. In this blog post, we will demonstrate how to record the network trace for mobile services using mobile backend tools.

First, login to your mobile services cockpit and ensure that feature "Mobile Network Trace" has been assigned to you mobile app:

Click the "Mobile Network Trace" feature, you will see the network trace histories inside the network trace view:

As you can see, there are already 2 network trace records for this mobile app. Let's take a look how to view the network trace records in mobile backend tools. 

First install SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools from VSCODE marketplace, and the follow the instructions to add the mobile service landscape to mobile backend tool from this blog post. Under the mobile app, open the "Network Traces" node:

Locate the network trace record you want to view and click "Download Network Trace" icon:

after the network trace record is downloaded, you should be able to view the content from the network trace analyzer:

And you can start recording the network trace from mobile backend tools directly without going to the mobile services cockpit. In order to do that, click the "Start Record Network Trace" icon in the Network Trace node:

You will see below the network trace recording UI:

If you want to include the payload in the network trace record, select "Header and Body" in the log message options and click "Start Recording" button:

Run you mobile app and perform the necessary requests to the server, and then click "Stop Recording" button on the UI:

Now you should be able to see the newly generated network trace record. Download the record and you should be able to view its content:

As a summary, in this blog post we demonstrated how to record the mobile services network trace using SAP Mobile Services Mobile Backend Tools. In addition to using the mobile services cockpit, you can record and view the network trace inside the Visual Studio Code environment without having to use third party tools.

entry.title: Re: Optimizing TM-EWM & TM-GTS integration by leveraging FEH resolution strategies
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog entry about this important topic!

You probably have a misleading type in 'When both “Target” & “Persistent” check box is enabled' as I think you meant the checkboxes 'Transient' (the systems tries to resolve the error symptoms ad hoc) and 'Persistent' (error symptoms are transferred to the Postprocessing Office (PPO) as postprocessing orders for later processing).

Could you maybe enhance the blog with some information?
For example where the error categories come from so it becomes clear how to steer for an individual enterprise service (interface) different problems, e.g., retry in case of locking issues, but no retries in case of master data issues.

entry.title: Re: Why is the automatic suggestion of an Account not exe...
entry.content:
Dear User, 

Like said below, when you are creating invoice without PO and product is free text (Items without master data), account determination will not happen automatically.

In the Business configuration work center - Implementation Projects - Open activity list - Fine Tuning - Activity - Edit Default Account Determination Group for Product Categories.

When you enter a supplier invoice or a goods receipt for a non-stock material or a service, you can specify just a product category without having to enter a particular product or service. Here, you can assign the account determination groups which the system shall use to determine the expense account to your product categories.

Here you can maintain the default account determination group for Materials and Services.

Best Regards,
Arun

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS 'Sparking Business Revolution through AI' (Virtual, 25 de Julio de 2024)
entry.content:
Virtual 

25 de Julio de 2024

Agenda

Estimado cliente,

Tenemos el placer de invitarle al Taller de Innovación SAP MaxAttention centrado en 'Sparking Business Revolution through Artificial Intelligence' que tendrá lugar virtualmente el 25 de julio de 2024. Aquí encontrará el programa actual del taller.

En un entorno en el que la inteligencia artificial está revolucionando el panorama empresarial, SAP le invita a un evento especial para descubrir cómo nuestras soluciones de IA pueden transformar su organización. Este evento contará con expertos de SAP Premium Hub CoE LAC, que compartirán estrategias y casos prácticos de uso de inteligencia artificial aplicados a diversos sectores.

Únase a los expertos de SAP Premium Engagement para conocer las capacidades de la suite SAP Business AI y cómo puede ayudarle en el camino hacia la excelencia y la revolución empresarial, con el respaldo de SAP MaxAttention.

Si desea inscribirse en este taller, póngase en contacto con maxattention@sap.com

Nuestros talleres

Estas sesiones y series de talleres se centran en un tema específico y su objetivo es permitir a nuestros clientes comprender la cartera de productos y las tecnologías innovadoras de SAP para impulsar su adopción y consumo. En concreto, ahora es más importante que nunca ayudar a las empresas a tener éxito y a responder a los mercados cambiantes transformándose en empresas inteligentes en la economía de la experiencia.

entry.title: Re: Assembly data cant be found in Work center POD.
entry.content: Hello Sayuj, I clarified and the following applies: The setting in the Manage Material application is the default that will be used if the data type in the BOM component is not maintained. However, to make the default work, the entry in the BOM component must be empty, and should not be "None". I tested and can confirm.

API
Application.moveWidget

Moves the widget into the first page canvas.
Parameters
widget:Widget

entry.title: TIME_OUT in t-code VA02 while deleting document
entry.content:
Hiya all.

We're getting TIME_OUT short dumps when deleting some SD docs via VA02. I traced the ST22, put a break-point in the exploding line, and found something weird.

The dump is raised within a LOOP in the DELETE_TEXT function module. While debugging, I found the system calls that function continually, with the it CATALOG containing one additional line each time the module is called.

I'm about to keep debugging that thing, but before that, I decided to try to get some hints from here. Maybe any of you has experienced that behaviour and can lend a hand before my brain melts.

I cannot promise I'll give you points for the right answer, but will do my best to be thankful and a good person enough to post my advances if I'll get any.

Thanks in advance,

Vic

entry.title: Re: C4P Project template

As of today the SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects doesn't offer such a functionality. Functionalities like

  • Project plan import from external systems (SAP S/4HANA Cloud or others)

Best regards

entry.title: BDT Erweiterung von CMS Immobilien Sichten
entry.content:
Guten Tag,

ich bin gerade dabei im Bereich CMS mithilfe von BDT Felder hinzuzufügen.
Im speziellen geht es um die CMS-Immobilien, Reiter "Gebäudeteile", Unterreiter "Beschreibung".

Hier gibt es folgende existierende Struktur im Bildaufbau:
-> Bild "RE0030 (Gebäudeteil - Beschreibung)"
    -> Abschnitt "RE0074 (Objekt Gebäudeteil - Beschreibung)"
        -> Sicht "RE0087 (Objektgebäudeteil - Bau/Versicherung Details)"
            -> Feldgruppen...

Ich möchte nun im Abschnitt RE0074 zusätzlich meine eigene Sicht hinterlegen:
-> Bild "RE0030 (Gebäudeteil - Beschreibung)"
    -> Abschnitt "RE0074 (Objekt Gebäudeteil - Beschreibung)"
        -> Sicht "RE0087 (Objektgebäudeteil - Bau/Versicherung Details)"
            -> Feldgruppen...
        -> Sicht "ZZ0087 (Meine Sicht)"
            -> Feldgruppen...

Obwohl ich das aber im BDT entsprechend customize sehe  ich meine Sicht nicht im CMS.

Ich habe auch versucht die bestehende Sich zu entfernen, aber sie wird trotzdem weiterhin angezeigt.
Das grundsätzliche Vorgehen meine ich verstanden zu haben, da ich bereits auf dem Reiter "Identifizierung" das gleiche gemacht habe und eigene Abschnitte hinzugefügt habe.

Das besondere hier scheint zu sein, dass der ganze Unter-Tabreiter wiederum Bilder enthält.
Das Bild "RE0030 (Gebäudeteil - Beschreibung)" ist auch als "Modal. Dialogfenster" gekennzechnet.
Ich weiß aber nicht was das bedeutet und ob das damit zutun hat, wieso ich meine Anpassungen nicht sehen kann.

entry.title: Re: Collaborative Project Management task assignment
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out to us. SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects offers such functionalities for work breakdown structures (WBS) in the project planning application. Today it is already possible to assign tasks to WBS elements and display them in the detail section within the project plan. 

Displaying Task in a Gantt chart is a known and documented functionality and will be delivered in future releases. 

Best regards

SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects

entry.title: A Simplified Guide to Effectively Use the Clearing API
entry.content:
Exploring the potential of the Clearing API Journal Entry – Clearing (Asynchronous) | SAP Help Portal can be an exciting journey for many of our customers. However, understanding its diverse functionality, distinguishing it from Fiori's clearing apps and preparing payloads might be complex. To ease this process, we've compiled this user-friendly guide. It provides clearer insights into the functions and capabilities of the Clearing API and guides you on how to use it effectively to simplify your financial transactions.

Business context:

Before we look into Clearing API, it's essential to know what Open Item Management and Clearing are.

Open items are incomplete business transactions. Clearing is the transaction when an offset value is posted to an item or a group of items, so that the resulting balance of the items is zero. Clearing transactions always create clearing entries.

To better understand, we take the examples in area of AR(Accounts Receivable), AP(Accounts Payable) and G/L(General Ledge).

 In AR accounting, the customer Invoice create open items. When make payments, these open items get cleared, resulting in a zero balance. For more information, please see Clearing of Open Items | SAP Help Portal

In AP accounting, vendor invoices generate open items. When make the payments, these open items get settled. Clearing result in a zero balance. More details can be found Clear Outgoing Payments | SAP Help Portal

In G/L - Consider an account like a bank transfer account. It remains an open item until a bank statement is obtained and cleared. More details can be found Clear G/L Accounts | SAP Help Portal  and Open Item Management for G/L Accounts | SAP Help Portal

Scope of the Clearing API:

The Clearing API is designed to manage the clearing of open items for the general ledger, accounts receivable, and accounts payable. However, it's important to note that this API only supports the clearing function and does not offer the ability to combine posting with clearing in a single step.

In accounts receivable, for instance, the customer invoice and incoming payment posting may not be automatically cleared. That's where Clearing API comes in. It clears the open items of customer invoices and incoming payments. Nonetheless, with the "Post Incoming Payments " app, you can post incoming payment and clear customer invoice in on step Post Incoming Payments | SAP Help Portal

Similar situation that for account payable, you can clear the outgoing payment and vendor invoice with Clearing API. But you can not post the payment and clear the vendor open item in one step, as app  Post Outgoing Payments | SAP Help Portal.

Using the Clearing API in Different Business Scenarios:

1.G/L Clearing:

  • Use case: Consider a situation where a bank transfer account is set up for open item management. In this scenario, when a payment is made, it gets posted to the bank transfer account as an open item. This open item only gets cleared when a corresponding bank statement is received and posted.
  • Sample Documents: Account 11001010 is defined as bank clearing account. Before clearing, there are 2 open items from Payment and  Bank Statement.After clearing, both open items are cleared and clearing document generated.
  • Sample Payload: Please provide the open items in the node <GLItems> 

 

<JournalEntry>
                    <CompanyCode>{{CompanyCode}}</CompanyCode>
                    <AccountingDocumentType>SA</AccountingDocumentType>
                    <DocumentDate>{{Today}}</DocumentDate>
                    <PostingDate>{{Today}}</PostingDate>
                    <CurrencyCode>EUR</CurrencyCode>
                    <DocumentHeaderText>Full Clearing</DocumentHeaderText>
                    <ReferenceDocument>Full Clear</ReferenceDocument>
                    <CreatedByUser>APITEST</CreatedByUser>
                    <GLItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <GLAccount>{{GL_OPEN_GLAccount}}</GLAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>{{Year}}</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{GL_Journal_Entry_01}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    </GLItems>
                    <GLItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>2</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <GLAccount>{{GL_OPEN_GLAccount}}</GLAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>{{Year}}</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{GL_Journal_Entry_02}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    </GLItems>
                </JournalEntry>​

 

2. AR/AP full Clearing:

  • Use case: In this scenario, the vendor invoice and its corresponding payment are both open items. The vendor invoice is fully cleared with the payment, which matches the invoice amount exactly.
  • Sample Documents: Before clearing, there are 2 open items for the vendor, one for invoice and one for payment.After clearing, both vendor invoice and payment are cleared, clearing document created.
  •  Sample Payload:

    Please put vendor invoice and payment open items in the <APARItems> Node

 

                <JournalEntry>
                    ………
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{PaymentEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    </APARItems>
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>2</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{InvoiceEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    </APARItems>
                   <APARItems>
                     ……
                  </APARItems>
                </JournalEntry>
​

 

3. Payment Differences - Partial Payment:

  • Use case: In an Accounts Receivable scenario, if a customer pays less than the invoiced amount, you'll need to address the payment difference. Both the original invoice and the partial payment remain as open items on the account until the complete payment is made. You can provide a reason for this partial payment, especially if you're using a predefined reason code. Ideally, ensure the 'Charge Off' indicator isn't active.  Similar situation for account payable. For more details please refer Partial Payments Versus Residual Items | SAP Help Portal
  • Sample Journal Entries: Before clearing, there are 2 open items, one for vendor invoice and one for payment.After partial clearing, open item for partial payment and invoice are not cleared. 
  • Sample Payload: You need to put  the vendor invoice and partial payment open items in the <APARItem>Node, and for invoice open item, please also provide partial payment amount in </PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy>

 

<JournalEntry>
                    ………
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{PaymentEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    </APARItems>
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>2</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{InvoiceEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    <PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy currencyCode=“EUR">4000</PartialPaymentAmtInDspCrcy>
                    </APARItems>
                   <APARItems>
                     ……
                  </APARItems>
                </JournalEntry>​

 

4. Residual Items with Payment Difference:

  • Use Case: If you post a residual item, the initial invoice and the payment are cleared and a new open item with reference to the original invoice is created. You can either enter your own short text to explain the reason or use a predefined reason code. Note that you must use a reason code for which the Charge Off indicator has not been set.
  • Sample Documents:  Before clearing open items for invoice and payment as following:After Clearing, New open item with residue amount generated.
  • Sample Payload: Please provide the payment difference amount in <OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy> and reason code in<PaymentDifferenceReason> , the reason code without Charge Off indicator.

 

                <JournalEntry>
                    ………
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{PaymentEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    </APARItems>
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>2</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{InvoiceEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                   <OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy currencyCode="EUR">-300</OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy>
                   <PaymentDifferenceReason>050</PaymentDifferenceReason>
                    </APARItems>
                   <APARItems>
                     ……
                  </APARItems>
                </JournalEntry>

 

5.Residual Items using Write-Off Reason Codes:

    • Use Case: Charge Off indicator has been set for the reason code you choose, the payment difference is charged off to a separate G/L account instead. For more details, please kindly check Reason Codes | SAP Help Portal.
    • Sample Documents: Before clearing, there are open items for invoice and payment.

    After clearing, both invoice and payment cleared, the difference post to sperate G/L account.

  • Sample Payload: 

 

                <JournalEntry>
                    ………
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{PaymentEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                    </APARItems>
                    <APARItems>
                        <ReferenceDocumentItem>2</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                        <AccountType>K</AccountType>
                        <APARAccount>1041</APARAccount>
                        <FiscalYear>2024</FiscalYear>
                        <AccountingDocument>{{InvoiceEntry}}</AccountingDocument>
                        <AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>
                        <OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy currencyCode="EUR">-1000</OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy>
                        <PaymentDifferenceDistribution>
                            <PaymentDifferenceReason>050</PaymentDifferenceReason>
                            <OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy currencyCode="EUR">-400</OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy>
                        </PaymentDifferenceDistribution>
                        <PaymentDifferenceDistribution>
                            <PaymentDifferenceReason>040</PaymentDifferenceReason>
                            <OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy currencyCode="EUR">-600</OtherDeductionAmountInDspCrcy>
                        </PaymentDifferenceDistribution>
                    </APARItems>
                   <APARItems>
                     ……
                  </APARItems>
                </JournalEntry>

 

This guide has been specifically designed to help you maximize the use of the Clearing API while minimizing potential issues, ultimately resulting in a more efficient accounting process. As we continue to evolve this guide, expect to see additional relevant business scenarios for a richer understanding and smoother financial journey.

entry.title: Advanced Valuation - How to Download a Result List into a Spread Sheet
entry.content:
To download the content of the results list, do the following:

  1. In the Schedule General Ledger Jobs app, go the job for which you want to download the results list.

     

  2. In the Results column, choose Download -> Text with Tabs, and then choose the results list that you want to download.
  3. The results list is downloaded to a .txt file.
  4. Open the .txt file and choose Edit ->Select All. Then, enter Ctrl+C to copy the selected data.
     

     

  5. Open a spreadsheet, for example, a Microsoft Excel file, and paste (Ctrl+V) the copied data into the file.

    Now, you can remove all line items that you´re not interested in and filter for those that you want to check.

entry.title: Tracking labor and machine time parallelly in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
In 24.05 release, SAP introduced new SAP Digital Manufacturing POD plugin Production time on / Production time off which refers to the duration during which a resource is actively engaged in producing a material, not including any idle time.

In this blog post, I will show you how to configure and use Labor & Machine time parallelly within SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Configure

To get started, in Manage Business Settings app, you need to enable Allow operator to track labor on Inactive SFC’s and Enable auto approval time records for Labor Tracking.

Next, you need to enable Report duration of Production time to ERP and Enable auto approval time records for Resource Time Tracking.

After configuring Manage Business Settings app, you need add Labor On / Labor Off plugins to your POD in POD Designer under Assign Actions button for an Action Button.

Labor On

Labor Off

As a next step, you need to add Production Time On / Production Time Off plugins to your POD in POD Designer under Assign Actions button for an Action Button.

Production Time On (Machine On)

Production Time Off (Machine Off)

Notice that, for both Labor On and Production Time On plugins, we have few flags and settings that can be adjusted i.e. for Labor On, Default Selection for Standard Value selected as Standard Value 3 and for Production Time On, Default Selection for Standard Value selected as Standard Value 2.

Use

Once all required configurations are in place, you can open your POD and try it out.

When an Operator starts work on a SFC, he will click Start button, then he will put Labor on the SFC using Labor On button. If he need to work on machine and record the machine time, he will click on Machine On button.

Start SFC

Labor On

Machine Start

All Time Tracking Records, for Labor and machine, can be seen in Manage Time Tracking app.

When an Operator completes his work on a SFC, he will click Machine Off button, then he will close Labor using Labor Off button. Lastly, he will Sign Off the SFC.

As a result of Auto Approve setting, time records will be auto approved and reported to ECC.

 

Conclusion

Production Time On/ Off capabilities were introduced to SAP Digital Manufacturing in 24.05 release, where operators can track time for Machine activities related to specific SFC along side the labor activities parallelly.

Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Thanks,
Swanand Joshi

entry.title: PDF Sending to REST API with JavaScript in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi folks, in this blog post I will show how PDF documents can be posted to REST APIs.

First we need to add a button then click on the button you added and open canvas logic editor.

First insert a file picker and select the file type you want to select.

Then insert a JavaScript script.

At the inputs field add your URL and Token by hand. Then add your file from output of your File Picker. My service returns me an ID attribute and I want to return it that’s why I added also an “id” variable to my outputs on the right.  

My full script is here:

 

let url = inputs.url
let file = inputs.file
let Token = inputs.Token
let path = await fetch(file[0].path)
let blob = await path.blob()

const formData = new FormData()
formData.append('file', blob, file[0].name)

var lv_options = {
  "schemaId": "*********************",
  "templateId": "***************************",
  "clientId": "default",
  "documentType": "custom",
  "receivedDate": "2024-05-06"
}
formData.append('options',JSON.stringify(lv_options))

const response = await fetch(url, {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    "Authorization": "Bearer " + Token
  },
  body: formData
})
if (response.ok) {
  let data = await response.json();
  return { id: data.id };
} else {
  alert("HTTP-Error: " + response.status);
}

 

Then I set the “id” attribute returned from POST response to an app variable.

It can be done with putting this formula to Assigned value section of Set app variable node’s properties:

 

outputs["POST"].id

 

And our app is ready to run. You can monitor POST request results on browser with F12.

Please comment on this blog post if you have any questions or recommendations.

entry.title: Introducing Dark CPI web extension For SAP Applications
entry.content:
Hello Readers,

This is my first blog. I want to share an open-source extension tool.

Extension provides control to change theme to dark mode for SAP CPI, SAP Build zone.
The Dark SAP CPI Web Extension is a versatile open-source tool that enhances the user interface of SAP CPI and SAP Build Work Zone. It offers a seamless transition to a dark theme, promoting a comfortable visual experience for users. In this guide, we'll show you how to use Dark CPI to get the most out of its features.

Key Features of Dark CPI

  • Ease of Use: Users can effortlessly switch to a dark theme, reducing eye strain.
  • Theme Variety: The extension boasts a range of themes such as 'Morning Horizon', 'Evening Horizon', and 'Quartz Light', allowing users to personalize their interface according to their preference and the time of day.
  • Accessibility: The dark theme can be activated via a simple click on the extension icon, making it easily accessible for all users.
  • Community Support: Currently, there are 80+ weekly users. Thank you for the support and feedback given by you all.

Installation Guide

Step 1: Download and Install from the Extension Store

  1. Go to the Extension Store: Chrome Store or EDGE Store (version 1.3 will be released very soon)
  2. Search for Dark CPI.
  3. Click on "Add to Browser".

Step 2: Enable the Extension

After installation, click on the Dark CPI icon in your browser toolbar.

Alternative Installation from GitHub

  1. Visit the Dark CPI GitHub Repository
  2. Download the latest release.
  3. Unzip the downloaded file.
  4. Open your browser and navigate to the extensions page (e.g., `chrome://extensions/`).
  5. Enable Developer Mode.
  6. Click on "Load unpacked" and select the unzipped folder.

How to Use Dark CPI

Popup on click appears as below under these conditions:
  • If you click on Extension Icon which is besides URL if extension is pinned, or you can pin it by clicking on extension manager of browser.
  • If active tab is not supported, 

  • It supported then you can see the name of application and current theme.

  • Launchpad supports theme are displayed in below buttons.

The options available are:
  • Default Theme: Provided by SAP.
  • Dark UI: Corresponding dark UI to the default theme.
  • Old UI: Rollback option (only visual, no functionality change).

Applying Themes

1. SAP CPI (from v1.0):

  •  Open the SAP CPI application.
  •  Choose your desired theme from the extension popup.
  •  This will enhance the SAP UI with the selected dark theme.

   Before Theme:

   After Applying Theme:

Design page of evening Horizon theme

   Rollback Theme (Old):

By default, the right-side navigation (home, monitor design, etc.) is open. Dark CPI will close it by default to avoid clutter.

  • Navigation Open: 

  • Navigation Closed:

2. SAP Build Work Zone (from v1.3):

  •  Launch the SAP Build Work Zone application.
  •  Select your preferred theme from the extension popup.

   It has user settings to switch theme. Keep same theme in extension popup.

   As you can see below, SAP does not support dark theme properly. This will enhance its UI.

   Before Applying Theme: 

   After Applying Theme: 

3. Theme Designer (from v1.3):

   - Open the Theme Designer.

   - Choose your desired theme from the extension popup.

   Before Applying Theme: 

   After Applying Theme:

Privacy and Transparency

The extension does not collect any personal data or tenant information, ensuring user privacy. Being open-source, users are encouraged to review the source code and monitor network activity for complete transparency.

Troubleshooting Tips

Theme Not Applying: Ensure the extension is enabled and you have selected the theme from the extension popup.

Visual Bugs: Report any issues on the GitHub repository for prompt assistance.

Conclusion

Dark CPI is your go-to extension for enhancing and customizing the look and feel of your SAP applications. With its comprehensive features, it caters to all your needs from theme customization to fixing SAP UI issues automatically. Install Dark CPI today and transform your user experience.

For more detailed information, visit the Dark CPI GitHub Repository.

entry.title: Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP - Installation
entry.content:
Setting up the Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP can significantly enhance your SAP testing framework, but it requires careful preparation. This blog post will guide you through the essential hardware and software prerequisites, detailed installation steps, and important configuration settings. By following this comprehensive guide, you will ensure a smooth setup process and maximize the potential of Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP, streamlining the management of test data, users, and projects.

Prerequisites

To use the Tricentis Test Automation Server, it must first be installed on the desired client. The client itself should fulfill the following requirements:

Hardware

CPUi5 Dual-Core 2.4Ghz
RAM16 GB
Hard disk space200 GB
Network100 Mbit/s

Software

Operating systems (64-bit is required)Win 7
Win 8.1
Win 10
Windows Server 2008 R2
Windows Server 2012
Windows Server 2012 R2
Windows Server 2016
Microsoft® .NET Framework3.5 or 4.8 
.NET Core Window Server Hosting*

2.2.3

3.1.0

User

The user of the machine on which you install Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP needs administrator rights.

 

Installation process

Once these requirements have been met, the Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP can be installed. It is important to ensure that the Test Automation Server for SAP that you want to install has the same version number as your installed version of Tricentis Test Automation for SAP. The current version is 2023.2.

During the installation itself, there is nothing else to consider apart from the "Service Configuration" item. In the "Service Configuration" you choose between the HTTP protocol and the HTTPS protocol and specify the desired port.

  • HTTP/HTTPS: Select an HTTP or HTTPS binding for the Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP features. If you want to protect your transferred user data, create an HTTPS binding for each service after you have installed Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP
  • Port: The default port is 80 for HTTP bindings, and 443 for HTTPS bindings. You can specify a different port. The port must be a value between 1 and 65535. You can automatically verify the availability of the port by entering a port number.

Once the installation has been successfully completed, the Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP is available.

 

Tricentis Test Automation for SAP Server landing page

The Tricentis Test Automation for SAP Server landing page is part of the installation. This webpage allows you to access all server features through a single web interface.

To access the webpage, type the following URL into a browser window: http://<server address>:<port>.
If you use an HTTPS binding , enter https://<server address>:<port>.

  • Replace <server address> with the name of the host or the IP address of the server.
  • Replace <port> with the port number you specified during the installation. This is only necessary if you changed the default port during the installation.

 

If the landing page is accessible, the installation has been successfully completed. Test data, users and projects can now be managed with the Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP.

Conclusion

In conclusion, successfully setting up the Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP requires careful attention to both hardware and software prerequisites, including a compatible operating system, adequate hardware specifications, and necessary user permissions. Following the installation steps precisely, particularly in configuring the HTTP/HTTPS protocols and port settings, ensures a smooth setup process. Once installed, the server’s landing page provides a centralized interface to manage test data, users, and projects efficiently. With everything in place, you're now equipped to leverage the full capabilities of Tricentis Test Automation Server for SAP, enhancing your SAP testing framework.

entry.title: How to get Tables used inside a t-code?
entry.content:
After an extensive search for a suitable program to effectively display program tables, I decided to take matters into my own hands. Through careful planning and execution, I've developed a small custom solution that streamlines the process of identifying tables necessary for generating tailored reports to meet the unique needs of each customer. I welcome any further suggestions or feedback on this endeavor.

Code listing for: ZCUST_TCODE_TO_TABLES
Description: Program to get TABLES used in a TCODE

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZCUST_TCODE_TO_TABLES
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Owner        : Surendra Karma
*& Note         : This is just a test program. I'm writing as per my
*&                understanding. This can not guarantee that all the
*&                tables are captured. Suggestions are always Appreciated!
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zcust_tcode_to_tables.

DATA: lv_tcode TYPE tstc-tcode.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_tcode FOR lv_tcode NO INTERVALS OBLIGATORY.
PARAMETERS : c_transp AS CHECKBOX,
             c_inttab AS CHECKBOX,
             c_view   AS CHECKBOX,
             c_append AS CHECKBOX,
             c_all    AS CHECKBOX DEFAULT abap_true.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  IF s_tcode IS NOT INITIAL.
    SELECT COUNT( * ) FROM tstc UP TO 1 ROWS WHERE tcode IN @s_tcode.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE 'Please enter a valid t-code' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  SELECT tstc~tcode,
         tstct~ttext,
         tstc~pgmna,
         d010tab~tabname,
         dd02l~tabclass,
         dd02l~sqltab,
         dd02t~ddtext
     FROM tstc AS tstc
     INNER JOIN tstct AS tstct ON tstct~tcode = tstc~tcode
     INNER JOIN d010tab AS d010tab ON d010tab~master = tstc~pgmna
     INNER JOIN dd02l AS dd02l ON dd02l~tabname = d010tab~tabname
     INNER JOIN dd02t AS dd02t ON dd02t~tabname = d010tab~tabname
     INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data)
     WHERE tstc~tcode IN @s_tcode
       AND dd02t~ddlanguage = 'E'
  AND tstct~sprsl = 'E'.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    SORT lt_data BY tcode tabclass tabname DESCENDING.
    IF c_all IS INITIAL.
      IF c_transp IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'TRANSP'.
      ENDIF.
      IF c_inttab IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'INTTAB'.
      ENDIF.
      IF c_view IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'VIEW'.
      ENDIF.
      IF c_append IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'APPEND'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  DATA lo_salv TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.

  TRY.
      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table   = lo_salv
        CHANGING
          t_table        = lt_data
      ).
    CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO DATA(lo_excp). " ALV: General Error Class with Message
      MESSAGE lo_excp TYPE 'E' DISPLAY-LIKE 'I'.
  ENDTRY.

  lo_salv->get_functions( )->set_all( abap_true ).
  lo_salv->get_display_settings( )->set_striped_pattern( abap_true ).
  lo_salv->get_columns( )->set_optimize( abap_true ).

  lo_salv->get_selections( )->set_selection_mode( if_salv_c_selection_mode=>row_column ).

  lo_salv->display( ).


*Selection texts
*----------------------------------------------------------
* C_ALL         All tables/structure
* C_APPEND      Append structures
* C_INTTAB      Internal tables/Structures
* C_TRANSP      Transparent tables
* C_VIEW        Views
* S_TCODE       Transaction Codes


*Messages
*----------------------------------------------------------
* Message class: Hard coded

Extracted by Direct Download Enterprise version 1.3.1 - E.G.Mellodew. 1998-2005 UK. Sap Release 757

entry.title: The Carrot Catastrophe
entry.content:
I was once working with a client in the frozen foods industry, tasked with integrating their SAP S/4HANA system with Warehouse Management System (WMS). The project was in its final stages, and just before the go-live date, we were rigorously testing various scenarios to ensure everything would run smoothly. Little did I know, a seemingly minor error in my Groovy script would lead to a comedy of errors.

During the testing phase, we began sending delivery IDocs from SAP. The process seemed straightforward, and I focused primarily on the technical aspects, paying close attention to field names and unit codes. However, in my meticulous concentration on the code, I neglected to verify the actual data being transmitted.

To my utter disbelief, I soon received a phone call from a client representative. The normally calm voice was now tinged with frustration and incredulity. The reason? The company had inexplicably received an order for several thousand tons of carrots. Yes, carrots. Apparently, due to a misconfiguration in my script, the system had interpreted some field values incorrectly, resulting in an astronomical order of this humble vegetable.

The most amusing (and slightly mortifying) part of the story was that this erroneous order, worth only a few euros, had triggered the dispatch of dozens of trucks filled to the brim with carrots. Imagine this scenario is productive system, people standing before a veritable sea of orange, and dozens of trucks lined, ready to deliver an absurdly large shipment of carrots.

In the end, the situation was resolved without too much trouble. The errant script was corrected, the delivery orders were recalibrated, and the carrots were redirected. While the incident certainly caused a stir, it also provided a much-needed laugh in the otherwise serious world of software integration.

This experience taught me the invaluable lesson of double-checking data outputs, no matter how technical the process might be.

And of course, it left me with a funny little story that I’ll never forget - the day I accidentally ordered a mountain of carrots.

entry.title: Re: concatenate hrpad23 fields to validate with dbtablog...
entry.content:
Hello,

You can use Open SQL string functions without concatenation, that can suit your needs. For example

  SELECT
    h~mandt,
    h~adatanr
  FROM hrpad23 AS h
  INNER JOIN dbtablog AS d
  ON
     left( d~logkey, 3  ) = @SY-mandt AND
     h~adatanr = substring( d~logkey, 4, 10 )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_temp).

Another possibility is to use Native SQL, AMDP'S or ADBC so you can execute a more complicated query with HANA Native SQL.
Lastly it is also possible to just fetch the data in internal tables and use logic in ABAP to keep the records you want.

entry.title: Re: TIME_OUT in t-code VA02 while deleting document
entry.content:
From your description, it sounds as if you can at least replicate it easily enough. Have you worked with dynamic logpoints already which can be set up via TCode SDLP and can be created based on a breakpoint? If you put one at the problematic statement and try to capture potentially helpful field content in the logpoint you might be able to gain some more insights. It's for example possible to have it capture the call stack and other helpful "entities". The online description for SDLP is quite helpful.

Here is the code as I see it in our NW 7.50 EHP8 system with SP28:

*     ...Texte sofort loeschen..........................................
 WHEN SAVEMODE_DIALOG.
      DELETE FROM  STXH CLIENT SPECIFIED
       WHERE MANDT    =  CLIENT
         AND TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
         AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
         AND TDID     IN R_ID
         AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
      DELETE_COUNT = SY-DBCNT.
      DELETE FROM  STXB CLIENT SPECIFIED
       WHERE RELID    =  'TX'
         AND MANDT    =  CLIENT
         AND TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
         AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
         AND TDID     IN R_ID
         AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
      DELETE FROM  STXL CLIENT SPECIFIED
       WHERE RELID    =  'TX'
         AND MANDT    =  CLIENT
         AND TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
         AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
         AND TDID     IN R_ID
         AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.

*     ...Texte ueber Verbuchung loeschen................................
 WHEN SAVEMODE_POSTING.
      PERFORM IMPORT_CATALOG.
*     ...Aufnehmen Texte aus Datenbank in Textkatalog...................
      IF TEXTMEMORY_ONLY <> 'X'.                             "B20K015761
         SELECT * FROM STXH
          WHERE TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
            AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
            AND TDID     IN R_ID
            AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
            PERFORM READ_CATALOG USING STXH-TDOBJECT
                                       STXH-TDNAME
                                       STXH-TDID
                                       STXH-TDSPRAS.
            IF SY-SUBRC > 0.
*              ...Text noch nicht in Textkatalog........................
               ADD 1 TO DELETE_COUNT.
               CATALOG_CHANGED = TRUE.
               PERFORM INSERT_CATALOG USING STXH-TDOBJECT
                                            STXH-TDNAME
                                            STXH-TDID
                                            STXH-TDSPRAS
                                            FUNCTION_DELETE
                                            FALSE.           "B20K074874
            ENDIF.
         ENDSELECT.
      ENDIF.                                                 "B20K015761
*     ...Texte in Textkatalog bearbeiten................................
      LOOP AT CATALOG WHERE TDOBJECT =  OBJECT
                        AND TDNAME   IN R_NAME
                        AND TDID     IN R_ID
                        AND TDSPRAS  IN R_LANGUAGE.
         IF TEXTMEMORY_ONLY = 'X'.                           "B20K015761
            DELETE CATALOG.                                  "B20K015761
            CATALOG_CHANGED = TRUE.                          "B20K015761
            ADD 1 TO DELETE_COUNT.                           "B20K015761
         ELSE.                                               "B20K015761
            CASE CATALOG-FUNCTION.

Hope that any of this helps!

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: "Uncaught TypeError" during "EnrichFinance" Step in "Business Partner Creation Approval Process"
entry.content:
We have imported and testing "Business Partner Creation Approval Process" live process project from SAP Build Process Automation Store.

We are able to reach until "EnrichFinance" step without any issues.

But on "EnrichFinance" step, when we're trying to add Bank or Tax Data in My Inbox task, nothing happens.

There are no calls being made to any URL.

But in Console, we see errors when we are clicking on "+" button

For Bank Data -

For Tax Data -

Upon navigation to the referenced Component-preload.js, we see below line of code in failed state -

onAddTaxNumber: function() {
                        var e = this.getParentModel().getProperty("/BPRequest/FinanceData/TaxData");
                        e.push({
                            TaxCategory: "",
                            TaxNumber: ""
                        });
                        this.getParentModel().setProperty("/BPRequest/FinanceData/TaxData", e);
                        this.getParentModel().refresh()
                    },

Trigger to the process -

We are triggering process via API. Below is our payload -

{
  "RequestId": "1718213572",
  "BPRequest": {
    "BusinessPartnerCategory": "2",
    "BusinessPartnerName": "Bhupesh LLC",
    "RolesData": [
      {
        "BusinessPartnerRole": "Z001"
      }
    ],
    "AddressData": {
      "Country": "IN",
      "PostalCode": "400101",
      "CityName": "Mumbai"
    }
  },
  "RequesterDetails": {
    "RequesterUserId": "P000001",
    "RequesterFirstName": "Bhupesh",
    "RequesterLastName": "Yadav",
    "RequesterEmail": "<hidden>"
  },
  "internal": {
    "isRework": false
  }
}

 Please let us know if we are missing anything.
